Samsung launched its Galaxy S25 series with Qualcomm's Snapdragon 8 Elite processor globally, abandoning its own Exynos chip for flagship devices after years of performance complaints from consumers.
Meta launched Llama 3, its most capable open source language model, which it claimed matched or exceeded GPT-4 and Claude 3 Sonnet on key reasoning and instruction following benchmarks.

Pfizer withdrew its sickle cell gene therapy from the market after a patient death during post-approval monitoring, a significant setback that raised questions about the long-term safety profile of the treatment.

Novo Nordisk received FDA approval for a once-weekly insulin formulation that it claimed would significantly improve adherence compared to daily injections offered by competitors Sanofi and Eli Lilly.

Intel launched its Gaudi 3 AI accelerator at a price point 30 percent below comparable Nvidia H100 configurations, explicitly targeting cost-sensitive cloud customers who were struggling to secure Nvidia supply.
The SEC alleged that the hedge fund had engaged in front-running trades ahead of large block orders it was executing on behalf of pension fund clients, betraying a fundamental duty of care.
The FTC alleged that the pharmacy chains had colluded to suppress generic drug competition by sharing pricing information through a third party data analytics provider.
European regulators fined the streaming platform 500 million euros for failing to provide users with a genuine choice to opt out of personalized advertising under the Digital Services Act.

The central bank raised interest rates by 25 basis points for the second consecutive meeting, citing persistent core inflation that remained above its 2 percent target despite slowing economic growth.
The investment bank was fined 150 million dollars by the Federal Reserve for deficiencies in its anti-money laundering controls, the third regulatory action against the firm in as many years.

Baidu's Ernie Bot gained 100 million users within three months of launch, establishing itself as the dominant Chinese large language model application and putting pressure on domestic rivals Alibaba and ByteDance.
TSMC reported its highest ever quarterly revenue as orders from Nvidia, Apple, and AMD continued to outstrip its available advanced node capacity.
SoftBank's Vision Fund reported a 4.6 billion dollar gain after the combined recoveries from its Arm IPO and a series of late-stage startup write-ups more than offset losses in its WeWork position.
Cathie Wood's ARK Innovation fund recovered 40 percent after a two-year drawdown, as a rebound in Tesla and Coinbase — its two largest holdings — drove the bulk of the gains.
The mutual fund giant Vanguard surpassed BlackRock in total assets under management for the first time, a symbolic milestone in the long-running competition between the two index fund pioneers.
Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway reported record operating earnings driven by strong underwriting results at Geico and double-digit profit growth at its railroad and energy subsidiaries.

George Soros's family office disclosed it had exited its entire position in Nvidia and initiated a new stake in AMD, signaling a shift in conviction about which chipmaker would benefit most from the AI infrastructure buildout.

Sequoia Capital wrote down its position in Instacart by 40 percent ahead of the company's IPO, acknowledging that the grocery delivery market had grown more competitive and less profitable than initially projected.

Tiger Global sold its entire stake in Rivian at a loss of approximately 700 million dollars, one of several high-profile EV investments that failed to deliver the returns the hedge fund had projected.

Masayoshi Son announced plans to invest 100 billion dollars in US AI infrastructure over four years, with initial commitments directed at OpenAI, xAI, and a handful of undisclosed semiconductor startups.
JPMorgan Chase raised its dividend by 10 percent after passing the Federal Reserve's annual stress test with its highest ever capital buffer, signaling confidence in its balance sheet resilience.

Goldman Sachs announced it would exit its consumer banking business entirely, taking a 4 billion dollar writedown on Marcus as it pivoted back to its core strengths in investment banking and asset management.
Morgan Stanley reported record wealth management revenue, with net new assets reaching 95 billion dollars as it continued to gain share from smaller regional brokerages.
Citigroup's transformation plan showed early results as its expenses fell and its return on tangible equity improved for the third consecutive quarter under its new chief executive.

HSBC announced the sale of its Canadian retail banking operations to Royal Bank of Canada for 10 billion dollars, as part of its ongoing strategy to exit non-core markets and concentrate resources in Asia.

Nvidia's H100 chips were selling on the secondary market at four times their list price as hyperscale cloud providers and AI startups competed for limited supply, with AWS and Google paying the highest premiums.
Apple's Vision Pro headset sold out of its initial production run within hours of launch, exceeding early expectations despite its 3,499 dollar starting price and limited third-party app support.

Microsoft's Copilot reached 30 million daily active users within nine months of launch, faster than any previous Microsoft product and ahead of internal targets set when the service was first announced.
Tesla's Cybertruck sold out its first year of production within 48 hours of opening reservations, validating demand for the unconventional design despite years of skepticism from industry analysts.

OpenAI's ChatGPT reached 100 million users in two months, making it the fastest growing consumer application in history and forcing Google to accelerate the launch of its Bard AI assistant ahead of schedule.
